observations.recommendation:
1.7. We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer review and recalculate the
balances in the RAO to ensure accuracy of amounts therein to prevent presentation
of erroneous information in the SAOB and SCBAA.

2.10. We recommended that:
A. The Municipal Treasurer:
a. Submit immediately all the liquidation reports and supporting documents for the
subject cash advances; and
b. Henceforth, strictly observe the prescribed period of liquidation of cash
advances and ensure that all reports of disbursements are supported by complete
documentary requirements.

B. The Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of the liquidation report, immediately
records the transactions and submits to the COA within the first ten (10) days of the
ensuing month.

3.7. We recommended that:
a. The Municipal Assessor gathers data on the cost of the road lot component of each
of the Local Road Networks included in the Road Map of the municipality, as well
as those recorded in the books of accounts, and commences the establishment of the
LGU's ownership on the lots; and,
b. The Municipal Accountant adjusts the road network account based on the
reconciled results of the data gathered from the Municipal Assessor.

4.6. We recommended that the Municipal Engineer conduct a technical review of the
rehabilitated road infrastructure to determine the accurate amount to be
derecognized.

4.7. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the books of accounts
accordingly by derecognizing the carrying amount of the rehabilitated road
pavements.

5.20. We recommended that:
A. The Local Chief Executive creates a disposal committee that would be
responsible for the disposal of unserviceable PPEs.

B. The Municipal Accountant
a. Reclassify the assets erroneously recorded as Printing Equipment to the ICT
Equipment account; and
b. Make necessary adjustments to correct the accumulated depreciation of the
PPEs acquired after the 15th of the month.

C. The General Services Officer
a. Make a representation to the Provincial Government of Eastern Samar to
obtain the deed of donation and/or turnover documents for the two PPE
items booked without sufficient documentation;
b. Prepare and maintain Property Cards; and
c. Tag all properties with appropriate property identification tags.

6.6. We recommended that the management place defined internal controls in relation to
the various activities involving fuel consumption, including the preparation of the
required supporting documents and reports as prescribed by the Manual on Fuel
Consumption.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entry in the book of accounts to reflect the transfer of funds for specific purpose. Furthermore, to require the respective barangays to submit Liquidation Reports or Report of Disbursements as proof of project implementation and as the basis for the Municipal Accountant in making an adjusting entry for the liquidation of fund transfers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor shall enjoin:
a) The General Services Officer to properly accomplish the Inspection and Acceptance Report every time goods are actually received and services are rendered for the LGU so that the accounts payable taken up in the books of the Municipal Accountant as of year-end are corroborated;
b) Concerned personnel to comply with deficiencies and lacking supporting documents noted during the audit of payments for transactions enumerated in Table 1; and
c) All other concerned government officers and employees to properly accomplish the required forms to ensure that all disbursements of government funds undergo strict scrutiny based on the established procedures.
observations.reason: This deficiency was still observed in CY 2023 transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
a) The Municipal Treasurer ensures the timely remittance of collections by the Revenue Collecting Clerks/Liquidating Officer, and that all official receipts issued are fully accounted for and reflected in the reports; and
b) The Municipal Accountant conducts a thorough review of the reports from the Municipal Treasurer, to properly recognize income based on accrual accounting, and make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the errors noted above.
observations.reason: No adjustment was made during CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts transactions to COA immediately.
observations.reason: This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of the Notice of Suspension and/or Disallowance from the post-audit of the accounts and financial reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive as the approving officer of all grants of cash advances must require the Municipal Accountant to regularly update the monitoring of all cash advances and strictly implement the non-granting of additional cash advances to officers or employees with outstanding balances. Moreover, for the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to all officers and employees with outstanding balances as of December 31, 2022, copies furnished to the Commission on Audit in order to facilitate the latter in taking appropriate action for that matter.
observations.reason: Granting of CA despite the existence of unliquidated CA is still practiced; There are AOs in the list who are already deceased.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned department heads to ensure that the 20% Development fund is well-planned and effectively utilized to cater to the development needs of its constituents. We also recommended that the MBO prioritize the determination of the CY 2020 and prior years’ unutilized balance for the 20% DF, per PPA, to aid the planning committee in the optimization of the fund.
observations.reason: All projects were implemented except the construction of Nitch due to the unavailability of the EMB approval for the construction. We also recommended that the MBO prioritize the determination of the unutilized balance for the 20% DF, per PPA, to aid the planning committee in the optimization of the fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall enjoin the LDRRM Officer to:
a) Review the DTRs of the JOs before approving, particularly with respect to their time-in and time-out reports, to ensure that claims against government funds are legal and valid; and
b) Stop allowing a straight 7-day duty to the JOs assigned as rescuers, rather, schedule them on a rotation/shifting basis so that each of them will be provided with a rest day to consider the JOs’ well-being while performing services for the government.
observations.reason: Management is still undergoing revision of the manning in the LDRRM Office.
LDRRMO submitted an updated list of vaccinees.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal Accountant to:
a) Trace prior period cash in bank transactions to locate the differences found in audit; and
b) Prepare & submit the lacking monthly BRS for CY 2021 and thereafter in accordance with the requirements set-forth in Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011.
observations.reason: Due to lack of proper turnover from previous accountant previous year records cannot be ascertained. The accountant will try to reconstruct the previous records but it needs manpower support.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a) Examine prior year cash transactions to locate the differences found in audit;
b) Draw journal entry vouchers to record all reconciling items requiring adjustments and corrections in the general as well as subsidiary ledgers; and
c) Strictly observe the regular and timely submission of the BRS.
observations.reason: The accountant will try to reconstruct the previous records but it needs manpower support.

observations.recommendation: Require all concerned accountable officers to immediately liquidate all cash advances and that current cash advances for travel should first be settled before new ones be allowed to the same accountable officers in compliance with the provisions of the cited rules in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.reason: Demand letters will be sent to some who are no longer connected with the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate records or
registries for the 20% Development Fund to facilitate monitoring and
reconciliation of balances and prevent any errors in the balance presented in
the Statement of Comparison Budgeted and Actual Amounts concerning the
said fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall enjoin:
(a) The Accountable Officers to immediately issue the corresponding
accountable forms once collections are made in conformity with Paragraph
1 of Section 68 of PD No. 1445 and prepare the RCD daily at every end of
the collection and, in a sequential manner, and using the prescribed
format.
The Municipal Treasurer to conduct daily, a physical count of the
accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collectors against the
reported issued OR in the RCD and the Cashbook along with the remitted
collections.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary accounting
entries in the Trust Fund and General Fund books to effect the transfer of the items of
PPE incorrectly recognized in the Trust Fund books.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Inventory Committee
and other officers concerned to adhere to the procedures in the one-time cleansing of
the PPE for a reliable inventory report as a basis for the reconciliation by the Municipal
Accountant.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the General Services
Officer maintain the PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, using the
prescribed formats.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary correcting entry
for the above-mentioned misrecognition.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive through the Municipal Budget
Officer stop appropriating Intelligence Funds in the Annual Budget which has no legal
basis to prevent mismanagement of government funds.

We also recommended that the release of CF shall be through cash advance of the SDO
and liquidation is supported with documentary evidence of payments for submission
to the ICFAU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall enjoin:
(a) The MSWDO to ensure that the certification of payment to the beneficiaries
and other pertinent documents are duly signed before forwarding to the
Municipal Treasurer to hasten the liquidation of the fund transfers;
(b) The Municipal Treasurer to:
1) Submit the lacking payroll supporting the payment of stipends to
indigent senior citizens;
2) Ensure that approving officials have certified in the distribution list
after the release of the stipend has been made; and
3) Ensure that any excess fund has been returned to the source agency
as supported with official receipts before forwarding it to the
Municipal Accountant for liquidation; and
(c) The Municipal Accountant to double check the supporting documents of the
liquidation report and ensure that it has been properly recorded before
submitting it to the source agency and the audit team in compliance with
Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and Item 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2023-004.

We recommended that the:
(a) Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit to the Municipal Accountant the
liquidation report with supporting documents of the fund received for AICS
and BNS; and
(b) Municipal Accountant to be mindful of the reglementary period within which
to submit the liquidation report and its recording in the books of accounts as
provided for in Items 4.6 and 5.4 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Require the Department Heads to comply with the submission of the
Project Procurement Management Plans (PPMPs) so that the Bids and
Awards Committee Secretariat can consolidate these PPMPs into the
Annual Procurement Plan (APP). This is to ensure that all procurements
are adequately planned and scheduled based on the Agency’s needs.
b. Instruct the BAC Secretary to use the prescribed format for the APP to
properly lay down the schedule of procurement for the period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee, Chairperson to monitor the
submission of contract by the BAC Secretary to facilitate the timely review
and communication of any deficiencies that may be noted.
b. Require the BAC Secretary to prepare a checklist of documentary
requirements based on COA Circular 2009-001 and the IRR of RA 9184 to
ensure completeness of documents submitted to COA.
c. Enjoin the BAC Secretary to submit the contract and other procurement
documents pertinent to the above-mentioned projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget
Officer to present to the Local Finance Committee the unencumbered funds under the
20% development fund for possible reprogramming to other identified priority
development projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant accurately prepare, compute and update the Local Road Network Ledger Cards and ensure that the Road Network account in the Financial Statements is verifiable to said records. Moreover, reconcile the identified properties during the physical count conducted as of December 31, 2022, to those recorded in the books and make adjusting entries, if necessary.
We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records accurately and that the claims are with proper and complete documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant revisit the accounting entries made to record the expenditures in the implementation of projects sourced from Fund Transfers from NGAs and to make the proper adjusting entries to reflect the status of these funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to ascertain and determine all transactions affecting the Trust Liabilities-LDRRMF account and the LDRRM fund itself at year-end and make the necessary adjusting entries in order to arrive at an accurate balance of the account.

We recommended that the:
a) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer reconcile their respective records; and
b) Municipal Accountant disclose appropriately in the notes to financial statements any reconciliation resulting from the foregoing.

We recommended that the Head of Requesting Unit for every transaction ensures that all requests for obligation are supported with appropriate documents and furnished to the MBO for reference; and that MBO makes appropriate corrections on the RAAO.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor/Vice-Mayor and the Human Resources Management Officer to:
a) Review the support staff functions per office to determine appropriate manpower complement of said office and exercise prudence in the hiring of JO workers; and
b) Ensure compliance with the hiring process and required documents by HRMO, before JO employees officially report to work.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the LDRRMO to consider the minimum fund allocation for the Quick Response Fund (QRF) and 70% for the mitigation fund and for the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) as the signatory to the LDRRMFIP to review the mathematical computation to ascertain compliance with the required fund allocation. Furthermore, for the Local Chief Executive as head of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to properly plan and identify the Programs, Projects, and Activities (PPAs) to be incorporated into the LDRRMIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer stop charging expenditures without or in excess of the approved appropriation and use the LDRRMFIP in its Registry of Allotments and Obligations (RAO).

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to
make the necessary adjusting entry in the book of accounts to reflect the transfer of
funds for specific purpose. Furthermore, to require the respective barangays to
submit Liquidation Reports or Report of Disbursements as proof of project implementation
and as the basis for the Municipal Accountant in making an adjusting entry for the
liquidation of fund transfers.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant accurately prepare, compute and
update the Local Road Network Ledger Cards and ensure that the amount presented
for the Road Networks account in the Financial Statements is verifiable to said
records. Moreover, reconcile the identified properties during the physical count
conducted as of December 31, 2022, to those recorded in the books and make
adjusting entries, if necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant
reconcile their records at year-end to recognize accounts payable accurately and that
the claims are with proper and complete documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor shall enjoin:
a) The General Services Officer to properly accomplish the Inspection and
Acceptance Report every time goods are actually received and services are
rendered for the LGU so that the accounts payable taken up in the books of
the Municipal Accountant as of year-end are corroborated;
b) Concerned personnel to comply with deficiencies and lacking supporting
documents noted during the audit of payments for transactions enumerated in
Table 1; and
c) All other concerned government officers and employees to properly
accomplish the required forms to ensure that all disbursements of government
funds undergo strict scrutiny based on the established procedures.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to revisit the accounting entries
made to record the expenditures in the implementation of projects sourced from Fund
Transfers from NGAs and to make the proper adjusting entries to reflect the status
of these funds.

We recommended that:
a) The Municipal Treasurer ensures the timely remittance of collections by the
Revenue Collecting Clerks/Liquidating Officer, and that all official receipts
issued are fully accounted for and reflected in the reports; and
b) The Municipal Accountant conducts a thorough review of the reports from the
Municipal Treasurer, to properly recognize income based on accrual
accounting, and make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the errors
noted above.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant
to ascertain and determine all transactions affecting the Trust Liabilities-LDRRMF
account and the LDRRM fund itself at year-end and make the necessary adjusting
entries in order to arrive at an accurate balance of the account.

We recommended that the:
a) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer reconcile their
respective records; and
b) Municipal Accountant disclose appropriately in the notes to financial
statements any reconciliation resulting from the foregoing events.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal Accountant
to submit the accounts to COA immediately.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive as the approving officer of all grants
of cash advances must require the Municipal Accountant to regularly update the
monitoring of all cash advances and strictly implement the non-granting of additional
cash advances to officers or employees with outstanding balances.

Moreover, for the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to all officers and
employees with outstanding balances as of December 31, 2022, copies furnished to the
Commission on Audit in order to facilitate the latter in taking appropriate action for
that matter.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned department
heads to ensure that the 20% Development fund is well-planned and effectively
utilized to cater to the development needs of its constituents.

We also recommended that the MBO prioritize the determination of the CY 2020 and
prior years’ unutilized balance for the 20% DF, per PPA, to aid the planning
committee in the optimization of the fund.

We recommended that the:
a) Head of Requesting Unit for every transaction ensures that all requests for
obligation are supported with appropriate documents and furnished to the
MBO for reference;
b) MBO makes appropriate corrections on the RAAO.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor/Vice-Mayor and the Human Resources
Management Officer to:
a) Review the support staff functions requirement per office to determine the
appropriate manpower complement of said office and exercise prudence in the
hiring of JO workers; and
b) Ensure compliance with the hiring process and required documents by
HRMO, before JO employees officially report to work.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall enjoin the LDRRM Officer
to:
a) Review the DTRs of the JOs before approving, particularly with respect to
their time-in and time-out and accomplishment reports, to ensure that claims
against government funds are legal and valid; and
b) Stop allowing a straight 7-day duty to the JOs assigned as rescuers, rather,
schedule them on a rotation/shifting basis so that each of them will be provided
with a rest day to consider the JOs’ well-being while performing services for
the government.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the LDRRMO to consider
the minimum fund allocation for the 30% Quick Response Fund and 70% for the
preparedness and mitigation fund of the 5% LDRRM Fund in the preparation of the
LDRRMF Investment Plan, and for the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator (MPDC) and Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) as the signatory to the
LDRRMFIP to review the mathematical computation to ascertain compliance with
the required fund allocation.

Furthermore, for the Local Chief Executive as head of the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to properly plan and identify the
Programs, Projects, and Activities (PPAs) to be incorporated into the LDRRMIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) stop charging
expenditures without or in excess of the approved appropriation and use the amount
of appropriations indicated in the LDRRMF Investment Plan in its Registry of
Allotments and Obligations (RAO).

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct:
a) The officers concerned to submit a written justification on the above-noted
findings and submit appropriate documents in support thereof, if necessary;
b) All end-users to stop undertaking procurement procedures to which they are
not technically and legally capacitated;
c) The Municipal Budget Officer to require appropriate documents for
realignment of funds before allowing charges to the LDRRM Fund; and
d) The end-user or implementing units to fully substantiate all transactions
where goods are to be distributed to constituents by providing a complete list
of recipients with a description and quantity of items/goods given/distributed.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) Examine prior year cash in bank transactions to locate the differences found in audit; (b) Draw journal entry vouchers to record all reconciling items requiring adjustments and corrections in the general as well as subsidiary ledgers; and (c) strictly observe the regular and timely submission of the BRS.
observations.reason: Previous year Bank Reconciliations are not finished.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive oblige the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the required details in the Notes to Financial Statements on the unexpended LDRRMF to substantiate the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and provide confidence to users of Financial Statements over transparency and accountability of its utilization.
observations.reason: Will make adjustment in the Annual Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Engineer on the immediate liquidation and settlement of any balances of fund transfers from DA-RFO 8. Also, require the Municipal Engineer to prepare Program of Work for the construction of irrigation canal that will supplement the rehabilitated impounding facility. And also, resubmit request for funding from DA-RFO 8 for the construction of the irrigation canal, otherwise, consider the same in the next appropriation law as priority economic development project, whichever comes earlier.
observations.reason: Committed to liquidate existing fund transfers and will coordinate and resubmit request funding with the DA-RFO 8 pertaining to the said project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU. Their reports will be reconciled with the book balances, and the differences should be properly accounted for. The periodic physical inventory shall strictly be conducted onwards after the initial count in compliance with Sections 102 and 111 of PD 1445 and COA Circular NO. 80-124. The GSO should be responsible for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU could be completely or accurately accounted for, and the unserviceable properties be properly accounted, disposed and recorded. Also, we recommend that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and update a schedule of depreciation expense showing the cost, acquisition date, useful life, monthly depreciation charges, accumulated depreciation and net book balance of each item of depreciable properties.
observations.reason: Valuation of counted PPEs are not yet determined.

observations.recommendation: Require all concerned accountable officers to immediately liquidate all cash advances and that current cash advances for travel should first be settled before new ones be allowed to the same accountable officers in compliance with the provisions of the cited rules in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.reason: Some of the unliquidated cash advances are from employees no longer connected with the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
1.11. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. Trace prior period errors comprising the unallocated differences in the
financial reports and make the necessary correcting entry so as to reflect the
correct balances and render reliable report for decision-making purposes by
its intended users; and,
b. Prepare & submit the lacking monthly BRS for CY 2021 and thereafter in
accordance with the requirements set-forth in Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445 and
COA Circular No. 96-011.

2.10. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct:
a. The Municipal Accountant to recognize expenses related to the inventories
already utilized or consumed to reflect in the financial statements the correct
inventory and expenses in accordance with the provisions in IPSAS 12 which,
in effect, will make the financial reports reliable for decision-making
purposes;
b. The Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to reconcile their
respective inventory records for the CY 2021 and thereafter and ensure
coordination so that movement of the inventories are duly accounted for in
accord with the provisions in Property & Supply Management System.
c. The GSO to coordinate with the Offices of the MSWD Officer and Municipal
Health Officer in-charge for the distribution of relief goods and drugs and
medicines under the control and supervision of the said offices, respectively,
so that updated record of inventory will be submitted to the Municipal
Accountant for reconciliation and recording in the LGU’s books.

3.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant
to strictly follow the recognition and derecognition of Inventories as laid out in IPSAS
12 to prevent possible misstatements in the financial statements.

4.10. We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant Designate to:
a. Maintain the required subsidiary ledgers for LDRRMF; and
b. Submit immediately to the audit team copies of the SL in LDRRMF and JEV
on the adjusting and closing entries related to the closing of the 2021 books of
accounts.

5.11. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Instruct the Local Development Officer to request from the Municipal
Engineer the project cost to support its budgetary requirement during the
planning and budgeting for the PPAs under the 20% development fund to
ensure that projects are fully funded; and,
b. Require the Department Heads to follow the preparation of PPMPs to
facilitate the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat in the consolidation of
these PPMPs into Annual Procurement Plan. This is to ensure that all
procurements are adequately planned and thus prevent the award of contracts
to which the LGU cannot comply due to budgetary limitations.

6.8. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards
Committee through its Chairperson to conduct the bidding process following strictly
the provisions laid down in the IRR of RA 9184 and relevant issuances to ensure the
integrity and propriety of the procurement process and in order that contracts are
awarded to eligible bidders.

7.11. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to adhere to the documentary requirements provided in the
COA Circular and other regulations before signifying its approval for payment in the
Disbursement Voucher in order that transactions are well supported to prove its
validity and propriety and to prevent the issuance of Notice of Suspension if evidence
warrants.

8.10. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards
Committee to strictly follow the reglementary period on the submission of contracts
to ensure timely review and compliance of any deficiencies that may be noted.

9.11. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to furnish the Municipal
Engineering Office (MEO) as to the contracts awarded to give the latter
information to base its monitoring of projects.
b. Instruct the MEO to conduct regular monitoring of projects and make a
demand if warranted to prevent delayed completion of projects which
hampers the attainment of desired community development. Likewise, to
prepare an Inspection Report especially for projects that are still on-going as
of the expected date of completion, and to compute for Liquidated Damages to
support deduction to any payments due to the contractor.

10.14. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct The Municipal
Accountant and the Mun. Budget Officer to:
a. Reconcile their records to arrive at a corrected Report on the Utilization of the
LDRRMF and disclosure on the Notes to Financial Statements, and submit the
same to COA; and,
b. Regularly reconcile their records on the LDRRMF to ensure correctness of
reports in the future.

11.13. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor shall enjoin the Municipal Accountant
to immediately submit liquidation report to the ECCD Council, as required in Section
4.6 of COA Circular No. 94-013 and the stipulations set-forth in the MOA.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to regularly reconcile their respective records to identify projects requiring adjustments to its proper PPE account and those necessary to be turned over to other LGU including the other items thereat into its appropriate classification and valuation.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to draw necessary adjusting journal entries to achieve proper valuation and appropriate disclosure of the Road Network account in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the provisions on the allowable advance payment in future similar transactions and adhere to the control measures on the grant of cash advances and imprest system principles.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of documents on funds utilization and liquidation on future similar relief operations to ascertain that government resources are properly applied for and appropriately utilized.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, the Head of the LDRRMC, ensure that updated and/or revised LDRRMFIP is duly approved before any charges or utilizations thereof to maintain regularity and validity of transactions in line with existing law and local ordinances.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and other officials concerned ensure to the compliance of the guidelines on the proper accounting of relief operations/distribution to ascertain receipt thereof by the targeted beneficiaries and eventually satisfy the primary purpose of the grant in responding future similar emergency situations.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal Treasurer to strategize and strengthen more its RPT collection drive by extending beyond the prescribed procedure of posting notices and take initiatives to reach the taxpayer’s attention over its respective obligations over realty tax in order to realize its goal and directly objective of providing more better services and facilities back to its constituents.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall ascertain that hiring of Job Orders be covered by the required contract of service and capacitate the designated HRMO for the screening and staffing to ascertain capabilities and skills of hired personnel for job at hand.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall emphasize the functions of the designated Human Resource Management Officer particularly on manpower planning, screening, selection, and staffing needs in order to achieve balanced and equally distributed tasks to job personnel.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Examine prior year cash in bank transactions to locate the differences found in audit;
b. Draw journal entry vouchers to record all reconciling items requiring adjustments and corrections in the general as well as subsidiary ledgers; and,
c. Strictly observe the regular and timely submission of the BRS.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to regularly reconcile their respective records to identify projects requiring adjustments to its proper PPE account and those necessary to be turned over to other LGU including the relation of other

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to draw necessary adjusting journal entries to achieve proper valuation and appropriate disclosure of the Road Network account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive oblige the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the required details in the Notes to Financial Statements on the unexpended LDRRMF to substantiate the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and provide confidence to the users of Financial Statements over transparency and accountability of its utilization.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the provisions on the allowable advance payment in future similar transactions and adhere to the control measures on the grant of cash advances and imprest system principles.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of documents on funds utilization and liquidation on future similar relief operations to ascertain that government resources are properly applied for and appropriately utilized.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive make representations to the Local
Sanggunian for the closure of the remaining dormant and unnecessary trust funds, and
revert the same to the general fund to sustain its programs or projects implementation for
the benefits of its constituents, and also achieve the fair presentation of the Cash in Bank
account in the financial statements

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strongly enjoin the Municipal Accountant
on the strict compliance with the required presentation and disclosure of the road network
in the financial statements to achieve reliability for decision-making purposes

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall formally request from the Borongan
MBC Merchandising and Construction the return of the amount erroneously advanced by
the LGU. And enjoin the Municipal Accountant to strengthen its internal control procedures
particularly on ascertaining the completeness and propriety of the supporting documents to
the disbursements. This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance, if
warranted

We recommended the strict adherence to the policies and procedures established for the
allocation and use of the LDRRMF particularly on highlighting LDRRMC’s role over crisis
situations to direct government funds to its proper and appropriate usage and disposition

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant on the
preparation of the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF and enjoin the
designated LDRRMO on the regular submission thereof on or before the 15th day after the
end of each month to COA Office to avoid delay in the delivery of audit results that supports
significant management decisions

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant
on the immediate liquidation and settlement of any balances of fund transfers from DA-
RFO 8. Also, require the Municipal Engineer to prepare Program of Work for the
construction of irrigation canal that will supplement the rehabilitated impounding facility.
And also, resubmit request for funding from DA-RFO 8 for the construction of the irrigation
canal, otherwise, consider the same in the next appropriation law as priority economic
development project, whichever comes earlier

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal Treasurer to
strategize and strengthen more its RPT collection drive by extending beyond the prescribed
procedure of posting notices and take initiatives to reach directly the taxpayer’s attention
over its respective obligations over realty tax in order to realize its goal and objective of
providing more better services and facilities back to its constituents
that Management shall prioritize the delivery of basic services and facilities for its constituents

that the Local Chief Executive direct the Planning and Development Officer in collaboration with the Budget Officer to make representations with the Local Sanggunian on the realignment of items included in the recapitulation of the priority projects and direct them for the attainment of desirable socio- economic development and environmental management that will have greater impact to the community consistent with the updated guidelines on its utilization
that the Municipal Accountant shall adhere to the disclosure requirements prescribed by the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards in order to achieve fair presentation of financial statements

that the Municipal Accountant shall ascertain from its records the details of the acquisition and disposal of the biological assets to facilitate the preparation of journal entry in derecognizing the biological assets no longer in the position of the agency with appropriate disclosures thereof in order to achieve reliable and faithful presentation of financial results

that the Municipal Accountant shall use the “Other General Services” account under Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses to record the wages of JOs except those that can be specifically identified related to the prosecution of civil works that an appropriate “Repairs and Maintenance” account will be used. Also, the Municipal Budget Officer shall maintain its registry and update the Local Chief Executive on the appropriation absorption capacity to accommodate additional temporary personnel so as not to exceed the limits established by law. And also, the Local Chief Executive shall be cautious with the limits provided by law to achieve balanced budget and fair presentation of financial results

that the Municipal Mayor shall monitor the compliance of the Municipal Accountant on the preparation and submission of the required monthly and quarterly financial reports, schedules and trial balances, including its supporting documents, in order to afford the LGU the earlier submission of the year-end financial results

that the Local Chief Executive shall ascertain that hiring of Job Orders be covered by the required contract of service and capacitate the designated HRMO for the screening and staffing to ascertain the capabilities and skills of hired personnel for the job at hand

that the Local Chief Executive emphasize the functions of the designated Human Resource Management Officer particularly on hiring and selection of Job Orders, and staffing needs in order to achieve balanced and equally distributed tasks to job order personnel

therefore, that the Local Chief Executive ascertain the proper reconciliation of the records between the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reflect the proper valuations and fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements. Also, the Municipal Accountant shall likewise be directed to prepare the following adjusting journal entries to establish the appropriate account title and amount of the ‘Cash Local Treasury’ account and derecognize the not applicable ‘Cash-Collecting Officer’ account while setting the proper tax revenue amount

that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to keep abreast with the recent amendments and developments in implementing the AICS program, particularly of the following: a. Gather collateral information about the client on life circumstances from immediate family members, relatives, referring parties and other sources, if necessary, to properly establish the eligibility of the client taking consideration the order of priority of targeted beneficiaries as dictated in the guidelines; b. Maintain objectivity, without bias, in determining the type, kind and amount of financial assistance to be recommended for approval of the appropriate authority; and, c. Direct and assist, as prescribed in the guidelines, those perennial indigent clients of the province, with chronic illnesses, for PhilHealth insurance coverage

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU. Their reports will be reconciled with the book balances, and the differences should be properly accounted for. The periodic physical inventory shall strictly be conducted onwards after the initial count in compliance with Sections 102 and 111 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 80-124. The GSO should be responsible for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU could be completely or accurately accounted for, and the unserviceable properties be properly accounted, disposed and recorded. Also, we recommend that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and update a schedule of depreciation expense showing the cost, acquisition date, useful life, monthly depreciation charges, accumulated depreciation and net book balance of each item of depreciable properties.
observations.reason: The LGU committed the implemented 01; by the end of 2015 – year all property and equipment are still in process recorded

observations.recommendation: Require all concerned accountable officers to immediately liquidate all cash advances and that cash advances for travel should first be settled before new cash advances be allowed to the same accountable officers in compliance with the provisions of the cited rules in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.reason: LGU did not make proper liquidation before the end of term.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management enjoined the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to comply with the regulation on the submission of contracts with complete supporting documents as required in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

Direct the LGU officials concerned to submit immediately the above listed reports with complete supporting documents. Henceforth, strictly comply the aforecited provisions of the law and regulations. Furthermore, the management should impose the applicable administrative and penal sanctions against the erring officials if warranted under circumstances.
observations.reason: The LGU committed to submit the required reports with complete supporting documents; submission of the report to COA has not yet been made.

Maintain objectivity, without bias, in determining the type, kind and amount of financial assistance to be recommended for approval of the appropriate authority; and, c. Direct and assist, as prescribed in the guidelines, those perennial indigent clients of the province, with chronic illnesses, for PhilHealth insurance coverage.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall adhere to the disclosure
requirements prescribed by the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards in
order to achieve fair presentation of financial statements

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall ascertain from its records the
details of the acquisition and disposal of the biological assets to facilitate the
preparation of journal entry in derecognizing the biological assets no longer in the
position of the agency with appropriate disclosures thereof in order to achieve
reliable and faithful presentation of financial results

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall use the “Other General
Services” account under Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses classification of expenses to
record the wages of JOs except those that can be specifically identified related to the
prosecution of civil works that an appropriate “Repairs and Maintenance” account will be
used. Also, the Municipal Budget Officer shall maintain its registry and update the Local
Chief Executive on the appropriation absorption capacity to accommodate additional
temporary personnel so as not to exceed the limits established by law. And also, the Local
Chief Executive shall be cautious with the limits provided by law to achieve balanced
budget and fair presentation of financial results

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor shall monitor the compliance of the
Municipal Accountant on the preparation and submission of the required monthly and
quarterly financial reports, schedules and trial balances, including its supporting
documents, in order to afford the LGU of the earlier submission of the year-end financial
results

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall ascertain that hiring of Job Orders
be covered by the required contract of service and capacitate the designated HRMO for the
screening and staffing to ascertain the capabilities and skills of hired personnel for the job
at hand

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall emphasize the functions of the
designated Human Resource Management Officer particularly on manpower planning,

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU. Their reports will be reconciled with the book balances, and the differences should be properly accounted for. The GSO should be responsible for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU could be completely or accurately accounted for, and the lapsing unserviceable properties be properly accounted, disposed and recorded. Also, we recommend that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and update a schedule of depreciation expense showing the cost, acquisition date, useful life, monthly depreciation charges, accumulated depreciation and net book balance of each item of depreciable properties.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016-01; 2015-01; 2014-09 The LGU made the inventory of all properties of the LGU. Their reports will be reconciled with the book balances, and the differences should be properly accounted for. The periodic physical inventory shall strictly be conducted onwards after the initial count in compliance with Sections 102 and 111 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 80-124. The GSO should be responsible for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU could be completely or accurately accounted for, and the lapsing unserviceable properties be properly accounted for, disposed and recorded. The Municipal Accountant to reconcile with the books. The accountant trace back the records until 2015 to come-up with the depreciation for the year and the current schedule. The schedule needs further improvement to add the property and equipment acquired on 2014 and the preceding years.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE prioritize the filling-up of vacant positions, particularly the Municipal Accountant to improve the services/outputs of the Accounting Office. Consider the creation of necessary positions, or the abolishment of non-necessary items in the LGU’s overall organizational set-up in order to maximize agency resources for the attainment of the municipality’s functions, targets and programs.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016-003 The LGU implemented the filling up of the position and improved the services/outputs of the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive enjoin all department heads involved in the development projects to monitor its implementation and to ensure that funds allocated for the 20% EDF be optimally utilized during the year in accordance with the time frame of project implementation that should be properly indicated in the Annual Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016-006 The concerned officials were enjoined to monitor implementation to ensure utilization as planned.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned officials to ensure that the Annual Budget details the specific priority projects identified by the Local Development Council under the 20% Development Fund. The LCE should also require that projects funded out the 20% EDF be implemented as planned or scheduled.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016-007 The concerned officials ensured that the Annual Budget details the specific priority projects and that projects funded from the 20% EDF be implemented as planned.

observations.recommendation: Require all concerned accountable officers to immediately liquidate all cash advances and that current cash advances be settled before new ones be allowed to be deducted in compliance with the provisions of the cited rules in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. AAR, Municipal Accountant will issue demand letter to all officials and employees who has unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015-02; 2014-02 The Local Chief Executive/municipal accountant advised and will pursue settlement. The LGU accountant will issue demand letters. For additional cash advances accumulated after this AAR, the Municipal Accountant will pursue through deductions of credit leave.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to comply with the regulation on the submission of contracts with complete supporting documents as required in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015-03; 2014-02 The BAC enjoined to comply with submission of complete supporting documents as required. Since then, BAC improved its compliance.

observations.recommendation: Direct the LGU officials concerned to submit immediately the above listed disbursement reports with complete supporting documents. Henceforth, strictly comply the aforecited provisions of the law and regulations. Furthermore, the management should impose the applicable administrative and penal sanctions provided for by the said provisions against the erring officials if warranted under circumstances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014-10 The LGU submitted reports thereafter and management imposed internal administrative controls. The LGU will comply with the provisions and impose sanctions if warranted.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, that the Local Chief Executive ascertain the proper accounting reconciliation of records between the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reflect the proper valuations and fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements. Also, the Municipal Accountant shall likewise be directed to prepare the following adjusting journal entries to establish the appropriate account title and amount of the ‘Cash Local Treasury’ account and derecognize the not applicable ‘Cash-Collecting Officer’ account while setting the proper tax revenue amount.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017-01 The LGU will adhere to the monthly reconciliation and make the proper adjusting entry.
observations.reason: Not Needed details of the understatement to make the adjusting entry.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry, as illustrated below, to book-up the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated LDRRMF balance in the General Fund to a Special Trust Fund under the account 'Trust Liability-DRRMF' in order to establish the same for the sole purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities within the next five (5) years.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017-02 The Municipal Accountant made the necessary journal entries and booked up the fund to Trust Liability-DRRMF to establish the balance for DRRMF activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive ensure the optimum utilization and completion of its Priority Projects funded from the 20% DF to attain the desirable outcomes of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017-06 The LGU ensured that remaining projects funded under 20% DF are being implemented and completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive ensure the 1% from the IRA to strengthen its Local Councils for the Protection of Children and to support the implementation of the programs, projects and activities of the latter for the welfare of the nation’s future human capital assets.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017-01 The LGU appropriated the 1% IRA and strengthened the Local Councils for the Protection of Children to support implementation of related programs and activities.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer shall intensify the collection of loan repayments by imposing the actual amount billed and consequently remit the same promptly to the GSIS. The practice of partial or non-deduction of loan amortizations from the respective payroll shall strongly be discontinued in adherence to the requirements of the GSIS Act that redounds on the benefits accorded to the active and separated personnel of the government.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017-05 Starting 2018 the payroll maker of the LGU implemented the policy to deduct in the payroll all the billing from the GSIS and remit the same promptly to GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Finance Committee to ensure that only GAD related PPAs be considered in the GAD Plan and Budget during budget deliberations with due considerations on the guidelines and prohibitions in the allocation and attribution as to serve the purpose of establishing the said GAD Fund and not redirect government financial resources from other priority projects of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017-06 The Finance Committee ensured that only GAD-related PPAs were considered in the GAD Plan and Budget and adhered to guidelines on allocation and attribution.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE prioritize the filling-up of vacant departmental positions, particularly the Accountant to improve the services/outputs of the Accounting Office. Consider the creation of necessary positions or the abolishment of non-necessary items in the LGU’s overall organizational set-up in order to maximize agency resources for the attainment of the municipality’s functions, targets and programs.

The GSO should be responsible for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU could be completely or accurately accounted for, and the unserviceable properties be properly accounted, disposed and recorded. observations.reason: On-going

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive enjoined the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to submit immediately the unsubmitted Disbursement Vouchers for calendar year 2016. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should submit the monthly reports together with the all vouchers, and payrolls in accordance with the provisions of Section 347 of Republic Act No. 7160 and COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned officials to comply with the regulations provided in COA Circular No. 2013-004 to uphold transparency and accountability. Also, the LCE should require the Municipal Engineer to inform our Office within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/project/activity that the appropriate projects signboards and/or public notices are already posted at the project site in order for us to validate the same.

We recommend that the LCE require the concerned officials:
a.) To stipulate in the Job Order Contract or Contract of Service the specific nature of work or duties and responsibilities and the desired work outputs of the JO personnel to facilitate comparison and evaluation of the accomplishments of these personnel.
b.) To submit copies of Job Order Contract or Contract of Service to the Auditor within five (5) days from its issuance as required in COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned officials to ensure that the Annual Budget details the specific priority projects identified by the Local Development Council under the 20% Development Fund. The LCE should also require that projects funded out the 20% EDF be implemented as planned or scheduled.
observations.reason: Lack of intervention of the LCE.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management adhere with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01 dated April 13, 2011 on the use of 20% Economic Development Fund. The Local Development Council should prioritize and incorporate in the Annual Investment Plan of the LGU those projects that are aligned with the stated regulation.

We recommend that the LCE direct personnel concerned to ensure that any expenditure out of the Special Education Fund be authorized by the Local School Board pursuant to an Approved SEF Budget as required under Section 99.b of RA 7160.

We recommend that the LCE direct personnel concerned to adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the current open dump site by fencing the perimeter area and assigning personnel to segregate biodegradable and non-biodegradable wastes for possible recycling. Also, Management must provide an adequate appropriation to fully implement its Solid Waste Management Plan and ensure effectiveness and sustainability of its Solid Waste Management Program. Barangays should be required to create Barangay SWM Committee and prepare and implement solid waste management policies.

We recommend that Management enjoined the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to comply with the regulation on the submission of contracts with supporting documents as required in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.reason: Contracts were still submitted beyond five (5) working days after execution of contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the BAC and other concerned officials to strictly comply with the provisions of Republic Act 9184 and it’s IRR, particularly, Sections 10 and 54.1 and Item 2(a), Annex “H” of the IRR.

We recommend that Management follow strictly with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 7, 2012 on the use of Local Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF). The LDRRM Council have to ensure that lined programs and activities under the 70% LDRRMF will directly address disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness issues in the locality and that the same be fully implemented during the year.

Direct the LGU officials concerned to submit 10 above listed reports with complete supporting documents. Henceforth, strictly comply the aforecited provisions of the law and regulations. Furthermore, the management must provide the applicable administrative and penal sanctions provided for by the said provisions against the erring officials if warranted under the circumstances.
observations.reason: Lack of intervention on the part of the LCE to compel the concerned officials to comply with the aforecited provisions of the law and regulations. Furthermore, the management must provide administrative and penal sanctions provided for by the said provisions against the erring officials if warranted under the circumstances.

of the LGU. Their reports will be reconciled with the book balances, and the
differences should be properly accounted for. The periodic physical inventory shall
strictly be conducted onwards after the initial count in compliance with Sections 102
and 111 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 80-124. The GSO should be responsible
for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU could
be completely or accurately accounted for, and the unserviceable properties be
properly accounted, disposed and recorded.

Also, we recommend that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to
prepare and update a schedule of depreciation expense showing the cost, acquisition
date, useful life, monthly depreciation charges, accumulated depreciation and net

order to maximize agency resources for the attainment of the municipality’s

to comply with the regulations provided in COA Circular No. 2013-004 to uphold
good local governance through transparency and accountability. Also, the LCE
should require the Municipal Engineer to inform our Office within ten (10) days
after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the
program/project/activity that the appropriate projects signboards and/or public
notices are already posted at the project site in order for us to validate the same.

a. To stipulate in the Job Order Contract or Contract of Service the specific
nature of work or duties and responsibilities and the desired work outputs of
the JO personnel to facilitate comparison and evaluation of the
accomplishments of these personnel.
b. To submit copies of Job Order Contract or Contract of Service to the

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive enjoin all department heads
involved in the development projects to monitor its implementation and to ensure
that funds allocated for the 20% EDF be optimally utilized during the year in
accordance with the time frame of project implementation that should be properly
indicated in the Annual Investment Plan.

to ensure that the Annual Budget details the specific priority projects identified by

should also require that projects funded out the 20% EDF be implemented as

expenditure out of the Special Education Fund be authorized by the Local School
Board pursuant to an Approved SEF Budget as required under Section 99.b of RA
7160.

We recommend that the LCE direct personnel concerned to adopt measures
to mitigate the effects of the current open dump site by fencing the perimeter area
and assigning personnel to segregate biodegradable and non-biodegradable wastes
for possible recycling. Also, Management must provide an adequate appropriation
to fully implement its Solid Waste Management Plan and ensure effectiveness and
sustainability of its Solid Waste Management Program. Barangays should be

The Local Chief Executive should create and Inventory Committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU. Their reports will be reconciled with the book balances, and the differences should be properly accounted for. Then, the Property Custodian shall prepare and update the Property Cards and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards. With these, regular reconciliation of accounting and property records could be undertaken. The periodic physical inventory shall strictly be conducted onwards after the initial count in compliance with Sections 102 and 111 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 80-124. The GSO should be responsible for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU will completely/accurately accounted for, and the unserviceable properties could be properly accounted, disposed and recorded.

Require all concerned officers to immediately liquidate all cash advances and that new ones be allowed only after previous advances be settled. Henceforth, strictly observe the provisions of the cited rules in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommend that Management enjoined the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to comply with the regulation on the submission of contracts with supporting documents as required in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommend that Management require the BAC and other concerned officials to strictly comply with the provisions of Republic Act 9184 and its IRR, particularly, Sections 10 and 54.1 and Item 2(a), Annex 'H' of the IRR.

We recommend that management observe the rules and regulations on the authorized deductions from the salaries of employees as provided in the General Appropriations Act issued every year before entering into Memorandum of Agreement to avail loans for its employees from private lending institutions. Let the individual employees remit their loan payments to these lending institutions.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive enjoined the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to submit immediately the unsubmitted Disbursement Vouchers for calendar year 2015. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should submit the monthly reports together with all vouchers, payrolls and official receipts in accordance with the provisions of Section 347 of Republic Act No. 7160 and COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommend that Management follow strictly with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 7, 2012 on the use of Local Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF). The LDRRM Council have to ensure that lined programs and activities under the 70% LDRRMF will directly address disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness issues in the locality and that the same be fully implemented during the year.
We recommend that Management follow strictly with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01 dated April 13, 2011 on the use of 20% Economic Development Fund.

Require all concerned employees to accomplish properly the required trip tickets when using vehicles, by using the prescribed form, and stating the specific purpose of the trip and the names of the authorized passengers, and indicating the estimated quantity of fuel consumed for the particular trip undertaken. Also require the General Services Office (GSO) to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption per vehicle supported with the approved trip tickets. Henceforth management should see to it that COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 and Section 4 (2 & 6) of PD 1445 be strictly followed by full coordination and supervision of the concerned officers and employees.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to submit the documentary requirements for Fund Transfers to Barangay in compliance with Section 23 of R.A. No. 7160, Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Henceforth, ensure that subsequent Fund Transfers are supported with complete documents/papers.

We recommend that Management require the payee of the cash advance and the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit/render all lacking documents to the Audit Team for verification purposes. Henceforth, Management should follow strictly the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 especially in the conduct of procurement through shopping or any of the alternative methods.

Direct the LGU officials concerned to submit immediately the above listed reports with complete supporting documents and required financial reports in relation to LDRRMF. Henceforth, strictly comply with the aforecited law and regulations.

We recommend that management submit the lacking documents and comply with the requirements of Section 362 of COA Circular No.91-368 whenever it undertakes minor repair in the maintenance of its service vehicles.
